info.title: Clients
info.description: Unified OpenAPI specification for clients domain

get.description:
Get a list of ClientNotes for a specific matter

Available for **Staff tokens**

parameters.name: matter_uid
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the client matter to retrieve notes for

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number of results.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: How many items to return per page. Maximum: 100

Note-Summary-ETA-Seconds.description: Estimated time in seconds until the AI-generated summary is expected to be ready. Only present when summary_status is 'in_progress'. Clients should poll again after approximately this duration. This is an estimate and actual completion time may vary.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid query parameters or malformed request

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions to access this resource

404.description: Not Found - The specified matter was not found

post.description:
Create a new note for a client matter

Available for **Staff tokens**

parameters.name: skip_summary_generation
parameters.description: If true, skips AI summary generation for this note. The note will be created with summary_status set to 'skipped'. Useful for batch imports, migrations, or when summary is not needed.

example.content: Client requested additional information about our service packages. Sent detailed pricing breakdown via email.
example.title: Service inquiry follow-up

Note-Summary-ETA-Seconds.description: Estimated time in seconds until the AI-generated summary is expected to be ready. Only present when summary_status is 'in_progress'. Clients should poll again after approximately this duration. This is an estimate and actual completion time may vary.

data.content: Client requested follow-up on proposal. Sent detailed pricing breakdown via email.

400.description: Bad Request - Malformed request or invalid syntax

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions to create notes

404.description: Not Found - The specified matter was not found

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - Validation error with valid syntax

errors.message: At least one of title or content must be provided

get.description:
Retrieve a specific ClientNote based on it's unique identifier (UID)

Available for **Staff tokens**

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the note

Note-Summary-ETA-Seconds.description: Estimated time in seconds until the AI-generated summary is expected to be ready. Only present when summary_status is 'in_progress'. Clients should poll again after approximately this duration. This is an estimate and actual completion time may vary.

data.content: Client requested follow-up on proposal. Sent detailed pricing breakdown via email.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions to access this note

404.description: Not Found - The specified note was not found

put.description:
Update an existing note for a client matter. At least one of title or content must be provided in the request. Additionally, the resulting note must have at least one of title or content non-empty after the update (e.g., you can set content to empty only if title remains non-empty).

Available for **Staff tokens**

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the note

example.content: Updated: Client confirmed they will attend the meeting on Thursday at 2 PM. No need for rescheduling.
example.title: Meeting confirmation

Note-Summary-ETA-Seconds.description: Estimated time in seconds until the AI-generated summary is expected to be ready. Only present when summary_status is 'in_progress'. Clients should poll again after approximately this duration. This is an estimate and actual completion time may vary.

data.content: Client requested follow-up on proposal. Sent detailed pricing breakdown via email.

400.description: Bad Request - Malformed request or invalid syntax

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions to update this note

404.description: Not Found - The specified note was not found

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - Validation error with valid syntax

errors.message: The resulting note must have at least one of title or content non-empty

delete.description:
Delete a specific ClientNote based on it's unique identifier (UID)

Available for **Staff tokens**

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the note to delete (e.g., "note_abc123xyz")

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions to delete this note

404.description: Not Found - The specified note was not found

get.description:
## Overview
Searches across business data entities including clients, conversations, bookings, appointments, estimates, payments, and documents. The search is powered by an Elasticsearch-based Seeker service.

The behavior varies depending on the `entity` parameter:
- **No entity or general entities**: Returns a combined result with `top_hits` grouped by entity type and `counts` per entity type.
- **`entity=appointment`**: Returns a decorated list of appointments with pagination support for recurring appointment series.
- **`entity=event_instance`**: Returns a decorated list of event instances with standard pagination.

### Rate Limiting
This endpoint is rate-limited to **60 requests per 60 seconds** per staff member. Exceeding the limit returns a `429 Too Many Requests` response.

### Staff Permissions
Search results are filtered based on the requesting staff member's permissions:
- Clients are scoped to the staff member's accessible clients unless they have full access.
- Payment and document results are hidden if the staff member lacks view permissions for those entities.
- Client email, phone, and last name visibility depends on the staff's model view permissions.

Available for **Staff and Internal tokens**.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search text to match against business data. Supports free-text search across entity fields (e.g., client name, email, phone number).

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description:
When set, restricts the search to a single entity type. The response format depends on the value:
- `appointment` — Returns a decorated list of appointments with recurring series support and specialized pagination.
- `event_instance` — Returns a decorated list of event instances with standard pagination.
- `client`, `conversation`, `booking`, `estimate`, `payment`, `document` — Restricts the general search to the specified entity type, returning the standard `top_hits` + `counts` response format.

When omitted, the search spans all entity types and returns the general `top_hits` + `counts` response.

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of entity types to include in the search results. Applicable when `entity` is not set. Possible values include: `client`, `conversation`, `booking`, `appointment`, `estimate`, `payment`, `document`.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination (1-based). For appointment searches with recurring series, this can also be an object with `virtual_count` and `physical_count` to properly paginate mixed physical and virtual appointments.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page.

parameters.name: quick
parameters.description: When set, performs a quick search with potentially fewer results but faster response time.

parameters.name: search_filter[text_filter]
parameters.description: Additional text filter applied within the search. Used for more targeted filtering beyond the main `query` parameter.

parameters.name: search_filter[staff_filter]
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of staff UIDs to filter results by specific staff members.

parameters.name: search_filter[from_filter]
parameters.description: Start date filter for time-based searches (e.g., appointments, events). ISO 8601 date format.

parameters.name: search_filter[to_filter]
parameters.description: End date filter for time-based searches (e.g., appointments, events). ISO 8601 date format.

parameters.name: search_filter[sort_type]
parameters.description: Field to sort by for appointment and event searches (used when `entity=appointment` or `entity=event_instance`). Forwarded to the Seeker service. The most common value is `start_time` to sort appointments by their start time. This parameter is only meaningful for entity-specific searches — for general search, use the top-level `sort_by` parameter instead.

parameters.name: search_filter[sort_order]
parameters.description: Sort direction for time-based results. Used in conjunction with `search_filter[sort_type]` for appointment and event searches. After Seeker returns results, the backend also uses this value to re-sort merged appointment lists (physical + virtual recurring instances) by `start_time`.

parameters.name: search_filter[payment_status_state_filter]
parameters.description: Filter appointments by payment status. Accepts an array of payment status values (e.g., "not_paid", "paid").

parameters.name: search_filter[tags_filter]
parameters.description: Filter results by tags. Used for tag-based client filtering.

parameters.name: search_filter[tags_relation]
parameters.description: Logical relation for multiple tag filters.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort results by for **general search only** (when `entity` is not set). The value is mapped through a column identifier mapping for client card fields, or used directly if no mapping exists. **Not applicable to appointment or event searches** — use `search_filter[sort_type]` and `search_filter[sort_order]` instead.

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction for the primary sort in **general search only** (when `entity` is not set). **Not applicable to appointment or event searches** — use `search_filter[sort_order]` instead.

parameters.name: secondary_sort_by
parameters.description: Secondary field to sort results by, used as a tiebreaker when primary sort values are equal.

parameters.name: secondary_sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction for the secondary sort.

parameters.name: select_all
parameters.description: When set to true, returns a slimmed-down version of client results suitable for bulk selection operations. Client first_name and last_name are excluded from the response.

parameters.name: new_decoration
parameters.description: When set to `true`, uses the newer matter-based client decoration format. The response will contain `counts` and `matters` instead of the default `top_hits` format for client results.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Filter results by specific tags.

parameters.name: sms_only
parameters.description: When set to `true`, filters results to only include SMS-related data.

parameters.name: score_sort
parameters.description: When set to `false`, disables Elasticsearch relevance score-based sorting. By default, score sorting is enabled when the business has the `score_sort` feature and the search text filter contains a space.

parameters.name: time_zone
parameters.description: Time zone to use for date/time calculations in search results (e.g., "America/New_York"). Defaults to the current user's or business's time zone.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Source identifier for tracking where the search request originated. Used for internal metrics.

200.description:
Search completed successfully. The response format depends on the `entity` parameter:
- **General search** (no entity): Returns `SearchResult` with `top_hits` and `counts`.
- **entity=appointment**: Returns `AppointmentSearchResponse` with decorated appointments.
- **entity=event_instance**: Returns `EventSearchResponse` with decorated events.

oneOf.description: Returned when no query parameters are provided or when the search returns no results.

401.description: Unauthorized. The bearer token is missing, invalid, or not allowed to access the requested resource.

404.description: Not Found. No matching results were found for the given entity search (appointment or event_instance).

429.description: Too Many Requests. The staff member has exceeded the rate limit of 60 requests per 60 seconds.

504.description: Gateway Timeout. The Seeker search service is unavailable or timed out.

get.description:
## Overview
Retrieves a paginated list of clients for the business. Supports searching by various attributes and filtering by update timestamp.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff, App, and Directory Tokens.**

parameters.description: Search Term
parameters.name: search_term

parameters.description: Search By - email/uid/phone/updated_at
parameters.name: search_by

parameters.description: Specify the updated_at filter range, available operators for updated_at: gte, lte. example: updated_at[gte]=2019-01-01
parameters.name: updated_at

parameters.description: Page number of results. Default: 1
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: How many items to return per page. Default: 25. Max: 100
parameters.name: per_page

200.description: Success - Returns list of clients matching the search criteria

429.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded for this business

post.description:
## Overview
Creates a new client record for the business. If a client with the same email already exists, returns a 409 Conflict error.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff, App, and Directory Tokens.**

email.description: Email Address. Must be a valid email format (e.g., user@example.com)

opt_in_transactional_sms.description: Whether the client opts in to receive transactional SMS messages.

staff_id.description: Staff ID. Reference to the staff member (see staff entity).

status.description: Use a client status to categorize clients according to their relationship with your business. Valid values: lead, customer, loyal, inactive

tags.description: The tag system is based on customized labels that you create, so you can sort your data using personalized words, phrases or themes that are meaningful to you and your business

put.description:
## Overview
Merge two or more duplicate clients into one primary client.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff, App, and Directory tokens.**

## Query Parameters
- `to_client_uid` (required): UID of the destination primary client
- `from_client_uids` (required): Comma-separated UIDs of clients to merge into the primary client

## Business Rules
- Clients must represent different contact persons to be merged
- Clients related to the same contact cannot be merged (returns 422 error)
- Use `GET /platform/v1/clients/merges/possible_merge_master` to check merge eligibility first

## Request Body
The request body can be empty but must be sent (e.g., `{}`).

parameters.description: UID of the destination primary client.
parameters.name: to_client_uid

parameters.description: UIDs of all the clients to be merged into the to_client_uid, as a non-empty comma-separated string (e.g., 'uid1,uid2').
parameters.name: from_client_uids

200.description: Success. Response includes master_ids (array of primary client UIDs) and clients (array of decorated client objects).

put.summary: Merge duplicate clients into a primary client.

schema.description: The request body. It can be empty but must still be sent.

get.description:
## Overview
Returns a list of client UIDs that can be used as a primary client for the merge.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff, App, and Directory Tokens.**

## Required Parameters
- `client_ids` (required): Comma-separated client UIDs to check for merge eligibility.

## Business Rules
- **Clients with multiple matters cannot be merged** - If any selected client has more than one matter/project, the merge is not allowed
- Returns 422 if multiple clients are related to the same contact (e.g., pets belonging to the same owner)
- Clients must be independent contacts to be eligible for merge
- The error message format is: '{Term} cannot be merged since you selected more than 1 {term} related to the same contact'

parameters.description: List of client UIDs to check for merge eligibility, as a non-empty comma-separated string (e.g., 'uid1,uid2').
parameters.name: client_ids

get.summary: List of clients that can be used as primary for merge.

post.description:
## Overview
Redeem a credit from the authenticated client's active package to pay for an appointment or service session. This marks the associated payment status as paid (price set to $0) and decrements one credit from the client's package. If multiple packages qualify, the **soonest-expiring package** is used first.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

## Required Parameters
- `payment_status_id` (string) — The ID of the payment status record to pay using a package credit. Must reference a pending/unpaid payment status linked to an appointment.

## Notes
- Returns 422 "There is no package to use" if no active client packages with available credits exist for the relevant service.
- Returns 422 if the payment status is already paid.
- There is also a Staff-facing version at `POST /platform/v1/payment/client_packages/update_usage` that accepts **Staff tokens** (and **Directory/App tokens** with the `X-On-Behalf-Of` header).
- This endpoint is in Alpha and may change.

200.description: Package credit successfully applied to the payment status. The payment status is marked as paid with price 0, and a booking credit redemption record is created.

422.description: Validation error - The request was valid but could not be processed. Common reasons include: no suitable package exists for the service, the payment status is already paid, or the client has no active packages with available credits for this service.

example.error: There is no package to use

500.description: Internal Server Error - May occur due to a known bug when the payment_status_id references a virtual booking that fails to be retrieved. The error message will contain 'undefined local variable or method `exception''. To avoid this, ensure the payment_status_id references a regular payment status that exists in the client's payment history.

example.error: undefined local variable or method `exception' for Components::Payments::ClientPackagesAPI:Module

payment_status_id.description: Payment status ID - Must reference a pending/unpaid payment status. Already paid statuses will return 422 error 'Payment status is already paid'.

get.description:
## Overview
Check whether the authenticated client has an active package with available credits for the service associated with a given payment status. Use this to determine if the "pay with package" option should be shown to the client before calling the `update_usage` endpoint.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

## Required Parameters
- `payment_status_id` (query, string) — The ID of the payment status to check. Must reference a pending/unpaid payment status.

## Response
- Returns `has_package: true` if a suitable active package with available credits exists for the service.
- Returns `has_package: false` if no suitable package is available.

## Notes
- Returns 422 if the payment status is already paid.
- This endpoint is in Alpha and may change.

parameters.description: Payment status ID - must be a pending/unpaid payment status
parameters.name: payment_status_id

200.description: Validation Occurred - Returns has_package=true if a suitable package exists

422.description: Validation Error - Common reasons: payment_status_id is already paid, or references an invalid payment status

500.description: Internal Server Error - Known bug: May occur when the payment_status_id parameter is missing, empty, or references a virtual booking that cannot be retrieved. The error message contains 'undefined local variable or method `exception''. To avoid this, ensure the payment_status_id is provided and references a valid payment status from the client's payment history.

example.error: undefined local variable or method `exception' for Components::Payments::ClientPackagesAPI:Module

Retrieves a Client.

**Available for Staff, App, and Directory Tokens.**

## Overview
Update a Client Available for **Staff, App, and Directory tokens**.

parameters.description: Client UID
parameters.name: client_id

address.description: Address. Send `null` or `""` to clear this field if force_nullifying is true.

created_at.description: Client creation date. Read-only - automatically managed by the system and ignored in updates.

email.description: Email Address. Must be a valid email format (e.g., user@example.com)

force_nullifying.description: If true, allows nullable fields (address, mobile_phone) to be cleared by sending `null` or empty string. Must be boolean true or string "true".

mobile_phone.description: Phone Number. Send `null` or `""` to clear this field if force_nullifying is true.

status.description: Use a client status to categorize clients according to their relationship with your business. Valid values: lead, customer, loyal, inactive

tags.description: The tag system is based on customized labels that you create, so you can sort your data using personalized words, phrases or themes that are meaningful to you and your business

updated_at.description: Client last update date. Read-only - automatically managed by the system and ignored in updates.

get.description:
## Overview
Gets a list of estimates for a client.

## Authentication
**Available for Directory tokens only.** Requires `X-On-Behalf-Of` header with the business UID.

**Note**: Staff OAuth tokens fail with 422 'Unauthorized' even with `payments.manage` permission due to authorization type mismatch in the backend. Use Directory tokens with `X-On-Behalf-Of` header instead.

**Note on Authorization Header**: Both `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and legacy `Authorization: Token <token>` formats are supported.

## Error Responses
Authorization failures return HTTP 422 with `{status:'Error', error:'Unauthorized'}` rather than 401/403.

parameters.description: A unique identifer of the business. Can be the Business UID or nickname\external ID
parameters.name: X-On-Behalf-Of

parameters.description: client's id
parameters.name: client_id

parameters.description: Filter by created_at, updated_at, state. state values: issued, approved, draft, invoiced, rejected. available operators for created_at and updated_at: gt, gte, lt, lte, eq. available operators for state: in. example: filter[created_at][gt]=2019-01-01, filter[state][in]=issued,rejected
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort by one of the following: created_at, updated_at, state. available operators: asc, desc. example: sort=created_at:asc,updated_at:desc
parameters.name: sort

422.description: Unauthorized - User does not have permission to access this client's estimates

get.description:
## Overview
Gets a list of invoices for a client.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff, App, and Directory Tokens.**

## Prerequisites
**Requires business feature `invoicing_features`** (payments module) to be enabled. Returns 403 Forbidden or 422 Unauthorized if the feature is not enabled.

## Error Responses
- **403 Forbidden**: Business does not have invoicing_features enabled
- **422 Unauthorized**: `{status:'Error', error:'Unauthorized'}` - Legacy error format for feature gate failures

parameters.description: client's id
parameters.name: client_id

parameters.description: Filter by created_at, updated_at, state. state values: issued, draft, declined. available operators for created_at and updated_at: gt, gte, lt, lte, eq. available operators for state: in. example: filter[created_at][gt]=2019-01-01, filter[state][in]=issued,draft
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort by one of the following: created_at, updated_at, state. available operators: asc, desc. example: sort=created_at:asc,updated_at:desc
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Page offset
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Page size
parameters.name: per_page

get.description:

422.description: Unauthorized - User does not have permission to access this client's payments

get.description:
## Overview
Get External Connected Payment Application.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

## Prerequisites
Requires a payment application (app_type=payments) to be installed and **fully connected** for the business. Returns 422 if no connected payment app exists.

### What "Fully Connected" Means
A payment app is considered fully connected when:
1. The app is assigned to the business via `POST /v3/apps/app_assignments`
2. The business has `external_[app_name]_connected=true` flag set
3. The app is not in pending/setup mode
4. For vcitaPayments: Terms and conditions must be accepted

description.text: This is a very long text that describes everything about the app

errors.message: To store cards on file connect to a credit card processor

post.description:
## Overview
Create New Card On File.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

## Prerequisites
Requires an active payment processor connection (Stripe, vcitaPayments, etc.) to be configured for the business account before cards can be stored.

default.description: Set as default card for this client
is default card (deprecated, use card.default instead)

parameters.description: uid of the payment with which the card was created
parameters.name: source_payment

get.summary: Get most recent pending card added to client [Alpha]
get.description:
## Overview
Get most recent pending card added to client [Alpha].

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

post.description:
## Overview
Create New Save Card Session. Creates a Stripe Checkout session for securely saving a card.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

## Prerequisites
1. Business must have the `cp_add_new_cof` feature flag enabled
2. Business must use Stripe as payment gateway (`payments_gateway_type: 'stripe'`)

## Supported Payment Gateways
- **Stripe**: Fully supported
- **Other gateways**: Not supported (returns 422)

success_url.description: URL to redirect to after successful card save

cancel_url.description: URL to redirect to if the user cancels

post.description:
## Overview
Creates a Cart.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

## Request Format
The request body must be wrapped in a 'cart' object with required fields: matter_uid, currency, and items array.

entity_type.description: The type of entity to add to the cart. Must be a capitalized Ruby model name. Common values for client carts: Invoice, Meeting, EventAttendance, ProductOrder, ClientBookingPackage, Cart. PendingBooking is also accepted but is typically used internally.

entity_uid.description: The identifier of the entity to add to the cart. The format depends on entity_type: for Invoice use the invoice UID string (e.g., "dg3g449g3gcdpmiz"); for ProductOrder it may be a numeric ID. Typically sourced from payment_status.payable_uid or the entity's own identifier.

matter_uid.description: Matter UID - the matter to associate with this cart

get.description:
## Overview
Show specific cart data.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the cart (e.g., "sg1oz2nuy0in41ly").
parameters.name: cart_uid

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the matter/conversation associated with the cart.
parameters.name: matter_uid

get.description:
## Overview
Retrieve a paginated list of client packages (package subscriptions) for the authenticated client, scoped to a specific matter/conversation. Returns each package's validity dates, credit balances, and active status.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

## Required Parameters
- `matter_uid` (query, string) — The UID of the matter/conversation to fetch packages for. Client packages are scoped to matters.

## Optional Parameters
- `page` (query, string) — Page number for pagination (default: 1).
- `per_page` (query, string) — Number of results per page (default: 25).
- `sort` (query, string) — Sort by one or more fields. Format: `field:direction`. Available fields: `created_at`, `updated_at`, `valid_from`, `valid_until`. Directions: `asc`, `desc`. Example: `sort=valid_until:asc` to show soonest-expiring first.
- `filter` (query, string) — Filter results by state. Format: `filter[state][eq]=ACTIVE` or `filter[state][eq]=INACTIVE`. ACTIVE packages have remaining credits and valid dates; INACTIVE packages are expired or fully consumed.

## Notes
- "About to expire" packages can be found by sorting by `valid_until:asc` and checking which packages have `valid_until` dates in the near future.
- An ACTIVE package has `valid_from <= now <= valid_until` and at least one booking credit with `total_bookings > bookings_usage`.

parameters.description: relevant matter_uid to fetch packages for
parameters.name: matter_uid

parameters.description: Page offset (default: 1)
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Page size (default: 25)
parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: Sort by one of the following: created_at, updated_at, valid_from, valid_until.  available operators: asc, desc. example: sort=created_at:asc,updated_at:desc
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Available filters: state: filter[state][eq]=ACTIVE/INACTIVE.
parameters.name: filter

get.description:

parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Page size (default: 25)
parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: Sort by one of the following: created_at, updated_at. available operators: asc, desc. example: sort=created_at:asc,updated_at:desc
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Available filters: entity_uid, entity_type. example: filter[entity_uid][eq]=123&filter[entity_type][in]=Invoice.
parameters.name: filter

get.description:
## Overview
Show specific deposit details.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

## Parameters
- `deposit_uid`: The unique identifier of the deposit
- `matter_uid`: (Optional) When provided, validated and used to filter by engagement; an incorrect value may result in not_found or missing deposit_uid rather than 422.

## Error Responses
- 404/not_found: When deposit does not exist or matter_uid (if provided) does not match.

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the deposit (e.g., "6y5ssai032sfpv54").
parameters.name: deposit_uid

parameters.description: Matter UID (optional). When provided, validated and used to filter; incorrect value may return not_found.
parameters.name: matter_uid

get.description:
## Overview
Show list of invoices.

## Authentication
**Available for Client Tokens only.**

parameters.description: Page offset (default: 1)

parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: Available filters: client_uid: filter[client_uid][eq]=123. matter_uid: filter[matter_uid][eq]=123. created_at: filter[created_at][eq/lt/lte/gt/gte]=2019-01-01. updated_at: filter[updated_at][eq/lt/lte/gt/gte]=2019-01-01. uid: filter[uid][in]=123,456
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort by one of the following: created_at, updated_at. available operators: asc, desc. example: sort=created_at:asc,updated_at:desc
parameters.name: sort

invoices.billing_address: 1 Bay St, San Francisco, CA, United States

parameters.description: The business UID (unique identifier string like '88pzdbz1hmkdoel4'). Note: Use the UID format, not the numeric database ID.

parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: Pass "true" to return only active packages. When omitted, all packages are returned.

200.description: Packages Info Retrieved with active packages only

get.description:
## Overview
Retrieve the list of available package catalog templates for a business, as seen from the client perspective. Returns packages that clients can browse and potentially purchase through the client portal.

## Authentication
**No authentication required.** This is a public endpoint.

## Required Parameters
- `business_id` (query, string) — The UID of the business whose package catalog to retrieve (e.g., "88pzdbz1hmkdoel4"). Must be in UID format, not numeric ID.

## Optional Parameters
- `page` (query, string) — Page number for pagination.
- `per_page` (query, string) — Number of results per page.
- `active_only` (query, string) — Pass `"true"` to return only active packages. When omitted, all packages are returned.

## Notes
- This is the client-facing view of the same catalog available to staff via `GET /platform/v1/payment/packages`.
- Only packages with `online_payment_enabled: true` are typically shown to clients for self-purchase.
- This endpoint is in Alpha and may change.

parameters.description: The unique identifier (UID) of the catalog package to retrieve.
parameters.name: package_id

get.description:
## Overview
Retrieve detailed information for a single package catalog template, as seen from the client perspective. Returns the package's pricing, included services, expiration settings, and discount information.

## Authentication
**No authentication required.** This is a public endpoint.

## Path Parameters
- `package_id` (required, string) — The unique identifier (UID) of the catalog package to retrieve.

## Notes
- This is the client-facing view of the same data available to staff via `GET /platform/v1/payment/packages/{package_id}`.
- This endpoint is in Alpha and may change.

## Authentication

## Required Parameters

parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort by one of the following: created_at, updated_at. available operators: asc, desc. example: sort=created_at:asc,updated_at:desc
parameters.name: sort

get.description:
## Overview
Get the checkout session of a given payment request. Available for **Client tokens**.

## Authentication
This endpoint is designed for client checkout flows and may also work without authentication for public checkout scenarios.

## Coupon Functionality
The optional `coupon_code` parameter applies a discount to the payment request. **Note:** Business must have `rollout.payments.coupons_checkout_v2` feature enabled and active coupons available. Returns 422 error if business is not eligible for coupon functionality.

parameters.description: Payment Request UID
parameters.name: payment_request_uid

parameters.description: Optional coupon code to apply discount to the payment request
parameters.name: coupon_code

post.description:
## Overview
Sends a message from the authenticated client to the business. The message direction is always `client_to_business` — it is set automatically by the server based on the client token.

The staff member who receives the message is auto-assigned based on the business's assignment rules and the client's existing relationships.

## Authentication
Available for **Client tokens** only. The client UID and business UID are extracted from the token — no need to pass them in the request body.

## Parameters
- `message.message` (required) — The text content of the message.
- `message.subject` (optional) — An optional subject line for the message.
- `message.entity` (optional) — Entity attachment that controls message type. When omitted, defaults to a plain text message. Set `type` to `"document"` and provide `uid` to attach an existing document.
- `matter_uid` (optional) — The UID of the conversation (matter/engagement) to add the message to. If omitted, the system uses the client's default conversation.

401.description: Unauthorized — invalid or missing client token.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity — validation error (e.g., missing message text, invalid matter_uid).

message.description: The text content of the message. Required.

subject.description: Optional subject line for the message.

entity.description: Optional entity attachment for the message. Controls the message type and, for document messages, attaches a file. When omitted entirely, the message type defaults to `text` (plain text message). The only supported entity type that triggers special behavior is `document` — all other values (including `text`) result in a standard text message with no attachment.

type.description:
The entity type, which determines the message type. Supported values:
- `"text"` (default) — A plain text message. No additional fields are needed.
- `"document"` — Attaches an existing document to the message. Requires `uid` to reference the document. The message will be linked to the document record and the staff member who owns the document will be auto-assigned if no other staff is resolved.

uid.description: The UID of an existing Document record to attach to the message. Required when `type` is `"document"`. The document must belong to the business associated with the client token.

object.description: Optional metadata string stored alongside the document attachment in the message data. Only used when `type` is `"document"`.

matter_uid.description: UID of the conversation (matter/engagement) to add the message to. If omitted, the system uses the client's default conversation.

post.description:
## Overview
Import clients to an app. Creates client records based on the provided field mappings.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff, App, and Directory tokens.**

## Prerequisites
1. The app specified by `app_code_name` must be assigned to the business
2. The app must have `app_type` set to `import_clients`
3. Use `POST /v3/apps/app_assignments` with `assignment_mode: 'internal'` to assign the app

## Request Body
- `import_params`: Configuration for the import process
- `fields_mapping`: Maps client fields to their values

parameters.description: App code name
parameters.name: app_code_name

422.description: Validation Error - Common causes include: app not installed, wrong app type, or invalid data

x-error-codes.not_installed: The application {app_code_name} is not installed for the business

errors.message: The application {app_code_name} is not installed for the business

500.description: Internal Server Error - May occur if request body is missing or malformed. Ensure import_params and fields_mapping are provided.

fields_mapping.description: Maps client fields to their values. At minimum, provide email or phone for contact identification.

send_notifications_when_done.description: Sends an email to staff when the import is done. Defaults to true. Set to false to skip.

upload_type.description: Is it single (individual contacts) or multi (multiple clients under the same contact)

get.description:
## Overview
Gets Matters of a client.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff, App, and Directory Tokens.**

parameters.description: Client UID
parameters.name: client_uid

post.description:
## Overview
Create a new Matter for a contact.

## Important Context
Creating a client via POST /platform/v1/clients automatically creates a first matter. This endpoint is used to create **additional matters** for an existing client.

## Name Field Requirement
Additional matters require a name field to differentiate them:
1. Call `GET /platform/v1/fields` to get field definitions
2. Find the field where `type == "name"` AND `object_type == "matter"`
3. Use the field's `id` as the `uid` with a unique name value

**Available for Staff tokens**

parameters.description: The UID of the contact (client) to create the matter for. This is the contact's unique identifier, also referred to as `contact_uid` internally.
parameters.name: client_uid

422.description: Validation Error - Common causes include: missing or invalid field UID, missing name field value, or invalid client_uid

500.description: Internal Server Error - May occur if the request body is empty or malformed. Ensure the request body contains the required matter object with fields array.

fields.description: Array of custom field objects to set on the matter. Must include at least one element: the matter 'name' field, which is required to create a matter. To obtain the correct field UID for the name field, call `GET /platform/v1/fields` and find the field where `type` is `name` AND `object_type` is `matter`. Use that field's `id` as the `uid` value in this array.

uid.description: The field definition UID. This is a dynamic identifier obtained from `GET /platform/v1/fields`. For the required name field, filter the response by `type='name'` AND `object_type='matter'` and use the `id` value (e.g., `hfv05wachtec1bvp`).

value.description: The value to set for this field. For the name field, this becomes the matter's display name and should be a descriptive, unique-per-client value (e.g., `Personal Injury Case`).

note.description: An optional note to attach to the newly created matter.

tags.description: An optional list of tags to associate with the newly created matter.

get.description:
## Overview
Gets Matters by filter. Searchable fields are: Email, First name, Last name, Matter name and Phone.

## Required Parameters
You must provide a `filter` parameter to search. Without it, the endpoint returns HTTP 422.

## Filter Formats
- `filter[advanced][start_with]=term` - Search by name/email/phone prefix
- `filter[uid_in]=uid1,uid2,uid3` - Get specific matters by UIDs

**Available for Staff, App, and Directory Tokens**

parameters.description: Filter query to search matters. Required. Examples: `filter[advanced][start_with]=john` (search by name/email/phone prefix), `filter[uid_in]=uid1,uid2` (get specific UIDs)
parameters.name: filter

put.description:
## Overview
Moves a matter from its current contact to a different destination contact (nesting). The source contact is automatically deleted if it has no remaining matters after the nesting operation.

## Important
The matter being nested must have a **different name** than any existing matter on the destination contact. If names collide, the API returns a 422 validation error.

## Authentication
Available for **Staff, App, and Directory tokens**.

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the matter to be moved/nested.
parameters.name: matter_uid

200.description: Matter successfully nested under the destination contact.

404.description: Contact not found - the destination contact_uid does not exist or does not belong to the business.

422.description: Validation error - typically occurs when the matter name already exists on the destination contact.

errors.message: A matter with this name already exists on the destination contact

put.summary: Nest a matter under another contact person

contact_uid.description: The unique identifier of the destination contact to nest the matter under. Must be a valid contact UID belonging to the same business.

put.description:
## Overview
Bulk add tags to one or more matters. This is the endpoint used by the product (e.g. with Staff token). Send `new_api: true`, `matters.uids` (array of matter UIDs), and `tags` (array of tag strings). Available for **Staff, App, and Directory tokens**. Requires packagable feature **tags_feature** (returns 401 when disabled).

401.description: Unauthorized - missing or invalid token, or missing tags_feature for the business.

new_api.description: Set to true to use the bulk API behavior (recommended).

uids.description: List of matter UIDs to add tags to.

tags.description: List of tag strings to add to the given matters.

delete.description:
## Overview
Bulk remove tags from one or more matters. Same contract as PUT: send `matters.uids` (array of matter UIDs) and `tags` (array of tag strings to remove). Optional query param `new_api: true` (Frontage sends it). Available for **Staff, App, and Directory tokens**. Requires **tags_feature** (returns 401 when disabled). Frontend: `frontage/app/assets/frontage/app/crm/actions/client-tags/client-bulk-tags-controller.js.coffee` (customDELETE with body).

parameters.name: new_api
parameters.description: Set to true to use bulk API (Frontage sends this).

401.description: Unauthorized - missing or invalid token, or missing tags_feature.

uids.description: List of matter UIDs to remove tags from.

tags.description: List of tag strings to remove from the given matters.

delete.description:
## Overview
Remove a tag from a single Matter (one matter_uid in path, one tag in body). For bulk remove use DELETE /v1/tags. Available for **Staff, App, and Directory tokens**.

parameters.description: Matter UID

delete.summary: Remove a tag from a Matter

get.description:

Gets a Matter.

**Available for Staff, App, and Directory Tokens.**

put.description:
## Overview
Updates a Matter.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff, App, and Directory tokens.**

## Validation
- `uid` (path parameter): Must be a valid matter UID that exists and is accessible to the authenticated user
- `fields[].uid`: Each field UID must be a valid field that exists for this specific matter. Use `GET /business/clients/v1/matters/{uid}` to see available field UIDs.

parameters.description: Matter UID. Must be a valid matter UID that exists and is accessible to the authenticated user.
parameters.name: uid

fields.description: A list of fields to be updated. Each field UID must exist for this specific matter.

uid.description: The field UID. Must be a valid custom_field that exists for this specific matter.

value.description: The field value to be updated

## Authentication

post.description:
## Overview
Create a new custom view - Create a new custom view - Available for **Staff, App, and Directory Tokens**.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: The format of the API authorization header should be: 'Authorization': "Bearer API_TOKEN"

level.description: View level. 'account' level views are visible to all staff members in the business, while 'staff' level views are private to the creating staff member.

view_type.description: Type of view. Currently only 'client' views are supported.

sorting_column.description: Column to sort by. Must be one of the allowed sorting column values.

filter.description: Filter configuration as a JSON-encoded string. Must be a valid JSON string that parses to either an object or array (e.g., "{}" or "[]"). Example: "[{\"key\":\"fields_filter.first_name\",\"value\":\"John\"}]"

get.description:
## Overview
Retrieve a specific view by UID.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff, App, and Directory Tokens.**

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: View UID

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: View type (system or custom)

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: The format of the API authorization header should be: 'Authorization': "Bearer API_TOKEN"

put.description:
## Overview
Update an existing view - Available for **Staff, App, and Directory Tokens**.

## Request Body Requirements
The `view` object must contain **at least one updatable field** (e.g., `name`, `level`, `columns`, `filter`, `description`, `order`, `pinned`). Sending an empty view object will result in no changes being made.

## Updatable Fields
- `name`: View name
- `description`: View description
- `level`: View visibility level ('account' or 'staff')
- `columns`: Array of column definitions
- `filter`: Filter configuration (JSON string)
- `sorting_column`: Column to sort by
- `sorting_direction`: Sort direction ('asc' or 'desc')
- `order`: Display order
- `pinned`: Whether the view is displayed/pinned

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: View UID

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: The format of the API authorization header should be: 'Authorization': "Bearer API_TOKEN"

view.description: View object containing at least one updatable field. Cannot be empty.

level.description: View level. 'account' level views are visible to all staff members in the business, while 'staff' level views are private to the creating staff member.

filter.description: Filter configuration as a JSON-encoded string. Must be a valid JSON string that parses to either an object or array (e.g., "{}" or "[]").

delete.description:
## Overview
Delete a custom view - Delete a custom view - Available for **Staff, App, and Directory Tokens**.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: View UID

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: The format of the API authorization header should be: 'Authorization': "Bearer API_TOKEN"

post.description:

Update multiple views in bulk - Update multiple views in bulk - Available for **Staff, App, and Directory Tokens**.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: The format of the API authorization header should be: 'Authorization': "Bearer API_TOKEN"

views.description: Array of view updates. Each item contains the view UID and the fields to update at the same level (flat structure, NOT nested under 'updates').

get.description:
## Overview
Get the client settings for a specific client.

## Authentication
**Available for Staff and Client Tokens.**

parameters.name: client_uid
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Client

put.description:
## Overview
Update the client settings for a specific client - Update the client settings for a specific client - Available for **Staff and Client Tokens**.

parameters.name: client_uid
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Client

matter_uid.description: Unique identifier of the client matter this note belongs to

content.description:
The HTML or text content of the note. May contain HTML markup for rich text formatting (e.g., "
some note here
"). Can be empty. At least one of title or content must be provided.

title.description: Title for the note. Provides a brief heading or subject line for the note content. At least one of title or content must be provided.

entity_uid.description: Unique identifier of the entity this note is associated with (e.g., document, payment, meeting). Optional - omit if the note is not linked to any specific entity.

entity_type.description: Type of entity this note is associated with. Required if entity_uid is provided. Possible values: document (note linked to a document), payment (note linked to a payment), meeting (note linked to a meeting).

content.description:
The HTML or text content of the note. May contain HTML markup for rich text formatting (e.g., "
Updated note content
"). Can be set to an empty string, but the resulting note must have at least one of title or content non-empty after the update.

title.description: Title for the note. Provides a brief heading or subject line for the note content. Can be set to an empty string, but the resulting note must have at least one of title or content non-empty after the update.

field.description: The field that caused the error

SearchResult.description: General search result containing top hits grouped by entity type and counts per entity type.

top_hits.description: Search results grouped by entity type. Each key is an entity type name and its value is an array of matching records.

counts.description: Total count of matching records per entity type.

client.description: Total number of matching client records.

conversation.description: Total number of matching conversation records.

booking.description: Total number of matching booking records.

appointment.description: Total number of matching appointment records.

estimate.description: Total number of matching estimate records.

payment.description: Total number of matching payment records.

document.description: Total number of matching document records.

ClientSearchHit.description: A client record returned from search results.

last_name.description: Client's last name. May be null if the requesting staff does not have permission to view last names.

full_name.description: Client's full name (first + last). Respects staff permissions for last name visibility.

email.description: Client's email address. May be null if the requesting staff does not have permission to view emails.

phone.description: Client's phone number. May be null if the requesting staff does not have permission to view phone numbers.

matter_uid.description: Unique identifier of the client's matter (conversation thread).

created_at.description: The date and time when the client was created, in ISO 8601 format.

color_id.description: A numeric color identifier derived from the client's UID, used for UI avatar coloring.

primary_staff_uid.description: Unique identifier of the primary staff member assigned to this client.

client_type.description: The type of client (e.g., regular client vs. demo client).

notifications_phone_display_value.description: Display-formatted phone number used for notifications.

client_data_associated_with_field.description: Custom client card field data associated with this client.

field_id.description: Internal ID of the client card field.

uid.description: Unique identifier of the client card field.

type.description: Type of the client card field.

value.description: Value of the client card field.

ConversationSearchHit.description: A conversation record returned from search results.

updated_at.description: The date and time when the conversation was last updated.

BookingSearchHit.description: A booking record returned from search results.

start_time.description: The start time of the booking.

AppointmentSearchHit.description: A decorated appointment record returned from appointment-specific search.

staff_id.description: Identifier of the assigned staff member.

staff_display_name.description: Display name of the assigned staff member.

is_recurring.description: Whether this appointment is part of a recurring series.

start_time.description: The start time of the appointment in the business's time zone.

start_time_utc.description: The start time of the appointment in UTC.

duration.description: Duration of the appointment in minutes.

client_id.description: Identifier of the client associated with this appointment.

client_color_id.description: Color identifier for the client's avatar.

no_show.description: Whether the client was marked as a no-show.

additional_staffs_uids.description: UIDs of additional staff members assigned to this appointment.

linked_booking_uid.description: UID of a linked booking, if any.

EstimateSearchHit.description: An estimate record returned from search results.

created_at.description: The date and time when the estimate was created.

PaymentSearchHit.description: A payment record returned from search results.

amount_h.description: Human-readable formatted payment amount with currency symbol (e.g., "$100.00").

invoice_number.description: Invoice number associated with this payment.

title.description: Formatted title combining header and invoice number.

created_at.description: The date and time when the payment was created.

DocumentSearchHit.description: A document record returned from search results.

direction.description: Direction of the document (e.g., "business" for outgoing, "client" for incoming). Note: internal "pivot" direction is mapped to "business".

created_at.description: The date and time when the document was created.

success.description: Indicates whether the request was successful.

appointments.description: Array of decorated appointment objects matching the search criteria.

count.description: Total number of matching appointments (including virtual recurring instances).

next_page.description: Pagination cursor for the next page. Can be an integer (simple page number) or an object with virtual_count and physical_count for recurring appointment pagination.

virtual_count.description: Number of virtual (recurring) appointments already returned.

physical_count.description: Number of physical (non-recurring) appointments already returned.

oneOf.description: Null when there are no more pages.

success.description: Indicates whether the request was successful.

events.description: Array of event instance objects matching the search criteria.

next_page.description: Next page number, or null if there are no more pages.

error.description: Error message describing what went wrong.

view_type.description: Type of view. Currently only 'client' views are supported.

level.description: View level. 'account' level views are visible to all staff members in the business, while 'staff' level views are private to the creating staff member.

filter.description: Filter configuration as a JSON-encoded string. Must be a valid JSON string that parses to either an object or array (e.g., "{}" or "[]"). Example: "[{\"key\":\"fields_filter.first_name\",\"value\":\"John\"}]"

sorting_column.description: Column to sort by. Must be one of the allowed sorting column values.

Bearer.description: Provide a valid bearer token in the Authorization header. Example: 'Authorization: Bearer {app_token}'

ClientMessaging.description: Provide a valid client bearer token in the Authorization header. Example: 'Authorization: Bearer {client_token}'

Notes.description: Provide a valid bearer token in the Authorization header. Example: 'Authorization: Bearer {token}'
